sous_titres.text: SHERYL: In Virginia, high school football is a way of life.
He worked so hard my mama left him,
Then the school board, forced us to integrate.
They combined the white school and the black school into one,
{\an8}SHERYL: That summer, a black teenager was killed
{\an8}And the city was on the verge of exploding.
{\an8}- This is my store! - Back off!
{\an8}Come on, Kirk, don't let Ray back you down like that!
{\an8}If they keep playing like that, we'll lose every game!
{\an8}I didn't think that was so bad.
Looks good, Gerry. I see you been working.
Listen, with the schools integrating and all,
some of the guys are worried about losing
Well, that's something we're just gonna have to figure out,
but you don't worry about that now.
You just keep at it. All right?
It's coming down at the store!
They want to burn the place up 'cause that coloured kid got shot.
{\an8}You just get Sheryl to the school now, Herb.
if you ever want to play for me again,
{\an8}You boys are gonna cool off, come with me to my office,
{\an8}Gerry, son, your heart's in the right place,
{\an8}but you ought to know better than to embarrass the coach like that.
{\an8}BERTIER: Hell, why don't you just kick them all off the team?
{\an8}I don't want to play with any of those black animals.
I'm Herman Boone. I'm the new assistant coach.
TYRELL: Well, from the looks of things,
I'd say we got all the help we need around here.
Why ain't you outside there with all your little friends, hollering?
This was the time that was arranged for me
Well, maybe you just better reschedule.
Coach Boone, the school board made the decision
to put you on my staff. I did not hire you.
Well, I came up here to coach at G.W.
I didn't ask the schools to redistrict.
I didn't ask to be assigned to your staff.
So I guess we're both in a situation we don't want to be in.
But I can guarantee you this, Coach.
Win? Coach Yoast here has been nominated
to the Virginia High School Hall of Fame.
I won a couple of titles down in North Carolina.
This here's Virginia. We play AAA.
What an opportunity for me, then.
{\an8}(I HEARD IT THROUGH THE GRAPEVINE PLAYING)
{\an8}WOMAN 1: No. Looks like they're moving in here.
{\an8}WOMAN 2: Lord, how many of them are there?
{\an8}MAN 2: It only takes one. Then we're gonna be overrun by 'em.
{\an8}NICKY: Mommy, where are you taking my teddy bear?
{\an8}MAN 3: Look there, here comes some more of 'em.
{\an8}WOMAN 3: That's Dr. Day, that Negro from the school board.
The school board has decided that Negro, Herman Boone,
is gonna be the head coach at T.C.
It's not fair! My daddy's head coach!
This is gonna be his Hall of Fame year!
And no one is trying to take away your daddy's future place in the Hall.
You can't just walk in here and take my daddy's job away!
That is enough, Sheryl. That's enough.
{\an8}It's all right, Bill. I feel the same way.
{\an8}It's those agitators and race-mixers I feel like taking a swat at.
{\an8}Troublemakers in the Berg are ready to put a torch to the city.
You want us to burn up like Watts?
Every head coach in the system is white.
We had to give them something.
It's the world we live in, God help us all.
BOONE: I left North Carolina because I was passed over
for a job that I had rightfully earned.
Gave it to a white coach down there.
Couldn't even tie up his own football cleats.
to do the same thing to this man?
Folks in Carolina say you marched with Dr. King.
Say you stood toe-to-toe with the Klan.
That's right. I'm also a family man.
black folks have never had anything in this city to call their own,
Herman, I think you'd better look outside.
So, you see, we need you, Coach Boone.
This here is my boy Julius.
He can be the finest defensive end
in the whole state if you push him to it.
I've heard a lot about you.
You're like an answer to our prayers.
Now, wait a minute. I'm not an answer to your prayers.
I'm not a saviour or Jesus Christ,
Martin Luther King, or the Easter Bunny.
I'm a football coach, that's all.
- Just a football coach. - You're our coach.
Gotten 11 offers and certainly no time for you.
Well, come on in. I've got an office out back.
BOONE: Look, I can't even spell diplomacy,
and I'm sorry about the way things went down,
but make no mistake, I am qualified to be this school's head coach.
Sure. You've been in, what, 4 or 5 programs in the past 10 years?
Well, I ain't gonna cook 'em and eat 'em.
The best player will play. Colour won't matter.
From the looks of our little situation we got us here,
I'd say it's about all that does.
We're in a tough spot, Coach, you, me, the whole city.
I think it would go a long way to smooth things over
if you would stay, work on the staff,
be a defensive coordinator, assistant head coach.
If that's the way you see it.
It's been a rare privilege to have lived here as long as I have
I'll be taking the year off...
After which I'll be moving to Loudoun,
taking the head coaching job at Loudoun High.
- I say boycott T.C. Williams! - Tell 'em, Fred!
Our boys aren't playing for some Coach Coon!
I started a petition, and I'm sitting this season out.
Only place you're going to sit is right back in that chair, Gerry.
MR. BOSLEY: Boycott T.C.! Boycott the school!
Stop this, Fred. You know that none of these boys
can afford to go to some other district just to play ball.
They sit this one out, they put their futures on the line.
- I ain't playing for no thief. - Don't do this.
Don't make this any harder for me than it already is.
Coach, if you go, I go.
PLAYER 1: I only play for you, Coach Yoast!
PLAYER 2: Don't go, Coach. You can't leave us.
ALL: Coach Yoast! Coach Yoast! Coach Yoast!
I've coached most of these boys since they were your age.
I've seen them grow up in front of my eyes,
almost like they were my own kids.
This is a heck of a time to be abandoning them
to look out for themselves, ain't it?
So, what are you gonna do?
PETEY: We gonna play some ball, y'all!
Black inside, black inside, black inside.
Yo, Petey, how many yards you figure you gonna get this season, bro?
You know, I ain't one to brag, big Blue,
but I figure on at least a thousand.
But I ain't one to brag.
Didn't I tell you all them white boys weren't gonna play for no brother?
From now on the Titans is gonna be powered by soul power, bro.
- JULIUS: Shut up. - You shut up.
- Yes, sir. - Why are you smiling?
First you smile, then you think.
You think football is still fun?
- It was fun. - Not anymore, though, is it?
It's not fun anymore. Not even a little bit.
Think, since you're thinking. Go on, think. Is it fun?
All right, listen up. I'm Coach Boone.
I'm gonna tell you all about how much
We leave for camp, Gettysburg College,
you will not be playing football this season,
You will wear a jacket, shirt, and tie.
If you don't have one, buy one.
Can't afford one, then borrow one from your old man.
If you don't have an old man,
then find a drunk, trade him for his.
'Cause I can guarantee you there isn't a burn on the street
that looks as raggedy and ridiculous
as what I'm looking at right now.
If you survive camp, you will be on the team.
I'm Coach Hinds, your offensive line coach.
Yell out your name and position starting from the right.
Yeah, yeah, yeah. Jerry Harris, quarterback.
And who in the name of heaven might you be?
Naval family, just moved here from Bayonne.
Someone said football, so I come running.
With him calling the shots, ain't none of us
gonna see nothin' but the bench this year.
He ain't calling the shots. You'll play.
You have just disrupted my first team meeting
This is my team now. Either you're with that or you're not.
- I'm here, ain't I? Let's talk football. - Let's talk football.
- I run the defence... - As a part of my team strategy.
Now, I have never seen an assistant coach's name
in the newspaper for losing a game.
I want a job for Coach Tyrell. He's been with me for 10 years.
I won't leave him out in the cold.
You don't get me without him.
All right. I will allow Coach Tyrell
to coach the special team, but I will have my eye on him.
Good morning, good morning, good morning, Coaches. How are you?
YOAST: Good morning to you. BOONE: Looks good today, doesn't it?
Just wanted to let you know what the offence is doing.
I run six plays, split veer. It's like Novocain.
Just give it time, always works. See you on the bus.
Be patient, Bill. Your time will come.
- Herman, here we go. - Here we go.
- How can I help you boys? - I'm Gerry Bertier,
the only All-American you got on this team.
You want any of us to play for you,
you reserve half the open positions for Hammond players.
Half the offence, half the special teams.
We don't need any of your people on defence.
Don't need none of my people?
What did you say your name was? Jerry?
No, you must have said Jerry, like Lewis,
which would make you Dean Martin, right?
Ladies and gentlemen, I got an announcement to make.
We got Jerry Lewis and Dean Martin
going to camp with us here this year.
Dean sings the songs and gets the girl.
Let's give them a round of applause.
- That's my mother. - That's your mama?
Take a good look at her.
'Cause once you get on that bus, you ain't got no mama no more.
You got your brothers on the team,
Now, you know who your daddy is, don't you?
Gerry, if you want to play on this football team,
you answer me when I ask you who is your daddy.
Is this your team, or is this your daddy's team?
Put your jacket on first and get on the bus.
All right, listen up, I want everybody off the bus.
Let's go. Follow me. Everybody. Let's go right now.
BOY 1: Why we got to get off the bus?
BOY 2: What are they doing, man?
All right, everybody, off the bus.
Listen up, I don't care, if you're black, green,
I want all of my defensive players on this side,
all players going out for offence over here.
Right now. Let's move! Let's move! Let's move! Let's move!
You and you, offensive bus. Sit together.
You and you, defensive bus. Sit together.
Because the person that I have you sitting next to
is the same one you'll be rooming with
for the duration of this camp.
I don't want to see your smilin' and shufflin',
and hear all your minstrel show singing on this bus.
I ain't lookin' at that for two weeks, man. Take it down.
You can close your eyes for two weeks, all I care.
Why don't you look at your wall and I'll look at mine?
I'll look wherever I want to,
and I don't want to be looking at that for two weeks.
Then you better use your X-ray vision, Superman,
and look right through it, 'cause it ain't coming down.
Get off me! Get off me!
You look like a bunch of fifth-grade sissies
You got anger. That's good. You're gonna need it, son.
You got aggression. That's even better. You're gonna need that, too.
But any little two-year-old child can throw a fit.
Football is about controlling that anger.
Harnessing that aggression into a team effort
Some coaches, they cut a player
if they think he's not up to snuff.
When they think he's hurting the team.
This is a public school program. I will never, ever cut a player
who comes out to play for me. But when you put that uniform on,
that Titan uniform, you better come to work.
in every aspect of the game.
You drop a pass, you run a mile.
You miss a blocking assignment, you run a mile.
and I will break my foot off in your John Brown hindparts.
And then you will run a mile.
Get up, boy! Get up! Get up!
Get up! Get up! Get up!
YOAST: Keep that head up! Keep those heads up!
BOONE: Everything we gonna do is changing.
We're gonna change the way we run.
We're gonna change the way we eat.
We're gonna change the way we block.
We're gonna change the way we tackle.
We're gonna change the way we win.
Get up. Get outta here. Go run. Go.
YOAST: Keep those legs movin'! Keep those legs apart!
Keep that ball with you till you get out of the briar patch.
Jump on that ball carrier like a starving man
What is wrong with you? Why are you fumbling my football?
- My blockers were bad. - Your blockers?
Your blockers ain't got nothing to do with you holding the ball!
Did your blockers fumble the football, or did you fumble the football?
- I did, sir. - All right. How many feet are in a mile?
How many feet are in a mile?
Five thousand two hundred and eighty feet!
You pick this ball up, you run every one of them!
You're killing me, Petey! You're killing me!
I see you eating lunch. But why you eating over here?
Why don't you go on over there and eat with your people?
I don't have any people. I'm with everybody, Julius.
Yeah, and I'm a dark-skinned cracker.
Come on, Julius, he's just another blessed child
BUCK: Lord, we come before you today
and ask you to soften big Julius Campbell's heart.
And Rev? He better be praying
I block for his black behind.
Yeah, but, Ray, if you don't block you're not gonna start.
I got some plays we won the city title with last year.
Got some trick plays, some stuff that might
fire up the boys' imaginations a little bit.
I think unless the boys start having a little fun,
they're not gonna put points up on that board for you.
You worry about your defence. Let me worry about the offence.
I want you to tell me something about one of your black teammates.
Sir, yes, sir! I'm rooming with Blue, sir,
and I noticed he wears those leopard-spotted underwears,
Okay, maybe somebody who's not your roommate.
Sir, I eat lunch with Rev.
People call him Rev 'cause he's always praying,
and he won't abide afoul tongue, sir!
And what is the Rev gonna do after high school?
Go to college and all that, I guess.
What about you? You gonna go to college?
Oh, not me, Coach. I ain't a brainiac like Rev.
Think you got a future in football?
Heck, no. I just figure if I got to be in school
I might as well hit some people while I'm at it.
A self-aware man, I like that.
But if you don't go to college,
it's not gonna be because you're not qualified.
So I want you to bring me your test scores
at the end of every week, and we'll go over them together, okay?
- Okay. - We'll keep that between you and me.
So, what kind of music does the Rev like?
LOUIE: Me and Rev both dig on The Temptations.
(SINGING) I know you want to leave me
But I refuse to let you go
Cause you mean that much to me
Each one of you will spend time every day
with a teammate of a different race.
You will learn about him and his family,
You will report back to me
until you meet every one of your teammates.
Until that time, we go to three-a-day practices.
You continue to ignore each other, we'll go to four-a-day practices.
Now, is there any part of this you don't understand?
Let's see. What's your daddy's name?
I mean, you do have a daddy, right?
I have a father and his name is Eric.
Wait, he does have a job, right?
I don't even have to ask, but I will.
What do you think of this one?
Does the term "cruel and unusual punishment"
Two minutes to go in the game.
The other team is out there, they're tired.
Fourth quarter. Fourth quarter. Fourth quarter.
LOUIE: Nobody from my family ever went to no college.
- I'll tutor you, Louie. - I'm white trash!
I ain't gonna get no C-plus grades! I'm just down-home, no-good,
never-going-to-no-college white trash, man! All right?
Would you hurry up and get off the phone?
- I got to call my girl, too, man! - Say it right now.
Oh, don't do this right now.
Mating habits of the mountain gorillas?
Gerry, you say it, or I'm not hanging up the phone.
BOY 1: Let's go! BOY 2: Watch it! Watch it!
YOAST: Get him! Get him! Get him!
44 stack! You're supposed to know your jobs!
Campbell, you're playing selfish, show-off football.
- Hold your man down. - Don't you single me out like that.
What, you think I'm stupid or something?
Listen, I'm the team captain, and I don't need you up in my face...
BLUE: We're here to play football, y'all. Let's play football.
Y'all didn't come out here to practice! 'Cause champions pay the price!
Lookin' like a bunch of burns out here!
BOONE: Let's go. Who's a coward? Cowards won't block.
BLUE: Coach, we need a water break. We been out here all day.
- I said we need a water break. - You need a water break.
Water is for cowards. Water makes you weak.
Water is for washing blood off that uniform,
and you don't get no blood on my uniform.
Boy, you must be outside your mind!
We are going to do up-downs
until Blue is no longer tired and thirsty.
There's a fine line between tough and crazy,
BOONE: All right, get some water.
Who you calling a boy, cracker?
Come on! You're all acting like a bunch of Sissies!
All right, man, listen. I'm Gerry, you're Julius.
Let's get some particulars and just get this over with, all right?
No matter what I tell you,
you ain't never gonna know nothing about me.
Listen, I ain't runnin' any more of these three-a-days, okay?
What I got to say, you really don't want to hear
'cause honesty ain't too high on your people's priority list.
All right, honestly, I think you're nothing.
Nothing but a pure waste of God-given talent.
You don't listen to nobody, man.
Shiver push on the line every time, man.
You can't run over everybody in this league,
and every time you do, you leave one of your teammates
hanging out to dry, me in particular!
Why should I give a hoot about you? Huh?
You want to talk about a waste, you the captain, right?
Captain's supposed to be the leader, right?
- You got a job? - I have a job.
- You been doing your job? - I've been doing my job.
Then why don't you tell your white buddies
to block for Rev better? 'Cause they have not blocked
for him worth a plugged nickel, and you know it!
I'm supposed to wear myself out for the team?
No. No, what I'm gonna do is,
I'm gonna look out for myself,
That's the worst attitude I ever heard.
BOONE: Let's go, let's go, let's go, let's go!
You will follow Doc, myself, and the other coaches.
We're gonna take a little run through the woods.
If you get lost along the way,
don't bother coming back to camp.
Just hitch-hike your hindparts on home.
Coach, this is a high school football team.
We're not in the marines here.
Come on, baby. Can't take no rest.
Anybody know what this place is?
This is where they fought the Battle of Gettysburg.
Fifty thousand men died right here on this field,
that we're still fighting amongst ourselves
Bubbling with the blood of young boys.
and hot lead pouring right through their bodies.
"I killed my brother with malice in my heart."
and you take a lesson from the dead.
right now, on this hallowed ground,
then we, too, will be destroyed.
I don't care if you like each other or not,
but you will respect each other.
learn to play this game like men.
We'll be out here all night, until we get this thing right.
- Let's go. - BERTIER: Let's go, team, pick it up!
- On the ball, Rev. - BERTIER: Come on, everybody.
BOY 1: Check Blue! BOY 2: There it is.
Whatever it is, it ain't blockin'!
You want a break? I'll give you a break!
Wait a minute, wait, wait. Let them handle this.
If they get to Rev once, just one time,
I swear to God, I'm gonna hit you so hard,
by the time you come to,
ooh, boy, you're gonna need a new haircut.
On the ball. Let's run it again.
You all right, big Petey? You all right?
Yeah, I love me a little contact, Petey!
BERTIER: What wins games? ALL: Defence!
BERTIER: What wins games? ALL: Defence!
Gerry, just keep your mouth shut
and get back on that field.
I just got transferred here from Huntington Beach, California.
This is my son Ronnie, and he's a quarterback.
How you doing, Ronnie? This is Coach Yoast.
We're pretty set at the quarterback, Colonel,
but, if the boy's any good,
you may want to check out Wilson or Hamilton.
He won't let blacks play on his team.
The way I see it, if these boys can fight a war together,
Now, he's a pretty good runner.
I think we can find a place for your boy.
PETEY: Hey, man, all that rubbin'
ain't gonna make them golden locks
grow back no faster now. You know that, huh?
That's cool. I can dig it.
SUNSHINE: Come on, now. Don't leave me hangin', bro. Come on.
BLUE: Hey, he got a little soul power.
I liked him better with the long hair, bro.
Now, how do you know what Bertier's mama looks like?
Brother, don't you know, me and Bertier's mama
went out on the town last night, bro?
- What are you say... - Hold up, hold up. Listen, now, Blue.
Why are you talking about Bertier's mama like that?
See, now, you making me angry, man.
Look like I ain't gonna be able to take your mama
What you laughin' at, Jerry Buck?
You need to tell your mama to shave them old legs of hers, bro.
maybe your mama can borrow the razor
and shave that old nasty back of hers.
You mad because she lookin' like a wolfman, bro.
Hey, hey. Lastik, man, what happened to you?
Man, I just gave your mama a piggyback ride,
and she weighs twice as much as I do.
(AIN'T NO MOUNTAIN HIGH ENOUGH PLAYING ON RADIO)
So, Julius, I was thinking that we...
You have no idea who you messed with, man!
There's too much male bonding going on in here for me.
To keep me from getting to you
I like that haircut. Get in there and show me what you got. Option eleven.

- Got to see the pitch, all right? - Yes, sir.
Now, you can throw it a mile, but you can't pitch it three yards.
Gotta see it. If you don't read it, you weave 'em.
JULIUS: You want this milk, Bertier?
- Hey, can you slide a bit, Gerry? - Hey, Ray, this is the defensive table.
It's not like I care or nothing, but being your roommate and all,
you was just messing with Bertier, right?
You was just messing with his mind, right?
What are you talking about, Petey?
Look, man, it don't matter to me, you know?
If, you know, if... I just got to know, you know?
If it don't matter, what's the big deal?
- I got to know. - Know what?
You know what I got to know? Don't mess with my mind!
Looks like Coach been keeping Petey out in the sun for too long, bro.
YOAST: Have my dessert. SHERYL: Coach Boone?
You did a good job up here.
You ran a tough camp, from what I can see.
Well, I'm very happy to have the approval of a five-year-old.
I'm nine and a half, thank you very much.
Why don't you get this little girl some pretty dolls or something, Coach?
- What is fatigue? - ALL: Army clothes!
- Will you ever quit? - ALL: No!
ALL: We want some more! We want some more!
Let me ask you something, Mr. Campbell.
What kind of power you got?
Oh, man, you know I got some soul power.
What kind of power you got?
What kind of question is that? I got soul power!
Let me ask you something now, Mr. Bertier.
- How strong are you? - I'm too strong!
Up in the front! Up in the front!
What'd they do up there, brainwash 'em?
They must be high on something.
- How you doing, sweetheart? - CAROL: I missed you so much.
Hey, man. Look, are we cool?
- Mrs. Boone, nice to meet you. - You, too.
This is Nicky. Nicky, that's Sheryl.
Why do you dress so weird?
Well, we're just all getting along, aren't we?
WOMAN 3: We don't want them here!
LOUIE: What you doing in my school, boy?
Man, this stuff is messed up.
and our season go down the drain.
But there are a lot of pretty women here.
Nah. Just a bad day. Things'll cool down.
No, Gerry. They're always gonna hate us.
We don't need you here, either, hippie boy.
Man, it's crazy out here, man.
Yeah, well, what did you expect?
I don't know. I ain't quite expect it to be like this.
Back to the real world, Bertier.
This is what they're integrating us for?
We'd have been better off staying where we were.
Yeah, this is my sanctuary right here.
All this hatred and turmoil swirling around us,
but this... This is always right.
It's just a game, Doc, but...
Coach, I'm afraid I've put you into a no-win situation.
I just found out the board didn't even think
you'd make it out of camp.
I've learned that at the first sign of trouble...
and turn it back over to Yoast.
They just expect me to pick up and move?
I don't know what to say, I...

Give us more. Do your stuff, let's go!
All right, everybody on their feet. On your feet, on your feet.
I'm not gonna talk to you tonight about
'cause you didn't kill each other up at camp.
Like all the other schools in this conference, they're all white.
They don't have to worry about race.
But we're better for it, men.
Let me tell you something. You don't let anything,
come between us. Nothing tears us apart.
the titans were greater even than the gods.

Well, that football field out there tonight,
takes the field for the first time

- Are all white girls that crazy? - What?
You guys can do better than that...
Alan, you've got to stick your man!

He's too fast, Gerry. I can't stick with him.

You want to play football or not?
Do you want to play football... Boy, get over there on the bench.

it don't do nothing but make me play worse.
I can't play for this man.
You come play linebacker for me.
23's killing us. I want you to get out there,
I want you to cover him for me.

I haven't practised with the defence. I can't.

I want you to cover 23. That's all you have to do.

- Do you? - Oh, come on, Herman.

He's just getting beat, that's all I'm saying.

The Titans are here, y'all, and we ain't going nowhere.
Yo, you got to be loud all the time, man?
